description_long: This is a tall, vertical wooden utility pole with two horizontal crossarms mounted near the top, each supporting three insulator caps. The pole is cylindrical and tapers slightly towards the top, with a natural brown wood texture and visible grain. The crossarms are arranged in a stacked configuration, one above the other, and are attached perpendicularly to the main pole. Each crossarm has three evenly spaced, dark-colored insulators, likely for supporting electrical wires. There are no visible wires, transformers, streetlights, or identification tags attached to the pole. The overall appearance is utilitarian and typical of power distribution infrastructure found along streets and roads.
description: A tall wooden utility pole with two stacked horizontal crossarms, each holding three insulators, and a natural brown finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the pole, both crossarms, and all six insulators clearly.
description_view_1: Side view showing the narrow profile of the pole and crossarms, with only the ends of the insulators visible.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both crossarms and all insulators.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the thin edge of the pole and crossarms.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Two horizontal crossarms are stacked vertically near the top of the pole, each perpendicular to the pole and parallel to each other.
